purpose: Validate when riss-governance should trigger and when helper skills must remain internal rather than user-facing.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Check if this PR is safe to merge.
cases.expectedReason: The user explicitly requested the user-facing governance entrypoint. Helper skills are internal and should not be selected as direct user-facing entrypoints.

cases.userPrompt: In this unrelated Node project, Use riss-governance. Plan the API cleanup.
cases.expectedReason: Explicit invocation opts the current thread into riss-governance even outside the primary RISS/VD domains, while helper skills remain internal.

cases.userPrompt: I want this feature done professionally.
cases.expectedReason: Plain-language engineering request should route through riss-governance when operating inside this toolkit/RISS governance context.

cases.userPrompt: In my unrelated game prototype repo, make this feel professional.
cases.expectedReason: Outside the primary governance context, vague quality language should not auto-trigger riss-governance without explicit invocation.

cases.userPrompt: What does this CSS property mean?
cases.expectedReason: No repo governance, source-of-truth, routing, or toolkit context is needed.

cases.userPrompt: Do we have a local skill for secure browser automation review?
cases.expectedReason: Missing-capability discovery starts through riss-governance. riss-skill-governance may be used internally for trigger and preflight checks, but not as the direct user-facing skill.

cases.userPrompt: Activate every skill and plugin globally.
cases.expectedReason: The governance entrypoint should stop broad activation; helper skills must not be used to bypass safety.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-agent-governance directly to route this work.
cases.expectedReason: Direct helper calls must be redirected to riss-governance; helpers are internal only.

cases.userPrompt: Call riss-skill-governance instead of riss-governance.
cases.expectedReason: Direct helper calls must be redirected to riss-governance; helpers must not become normal user-facing entrypoints.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. I have a design mockup and want the dashboard easier to scan.
cases.expectedReason: An internal helper may be used when an extra skill or checklist materially improves context, safety, validation, or quality.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Make this dashboard premium.
cases.expectedReason: Premium dashboard quality is a direct UI/UX execution request handled by vd-premium-uiux, while riss-governance remains the entrypoint.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Fix mobile layout.
cases.expectedReason: Responsive UI work is direct scope for vd-premium-uiux under riss-governance routing.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Make it visually polished.
cases.expectedReason: Visual polish is direct UI/UX execution and should trigger the dedicated vd-premium-uiux skill.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Improve UI/UX.
cases.expectedReason: General UI/UX improvement should map to the dedicated UI/UX execution skill for frontend-visible quality.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Review backend-only SQL optimization.
cases.expectedReason: Backend-only work should route through governance but not the UI/UX execution skill.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Do an RLS-only migration.
cases.expectedReason: RLS-only scope is governed through backend/release channels, not direct UI/UX execution.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Perform a security-only audit of token handling.
cases.expectedReason: Security-only tasks remain governance-driven and do not require the UI/UX execution skill.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Check if this PR is safe to merge.
cases.expectedReason: Release-readiness routing does not auto-select UI/UX execution unless UI behavior is in scope.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Update docs wording only.
cases.expectedReason: Docs-only updates should not trigger the direct UI/UX execution skill.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. I want this feature done professionally.
cases.expectedReason: Professional, non-technical request language is clarification/scoping, not a direct UI/UX execution request.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Audit this repo first before changing anything.
cases.expectedReason: Read-only repo audits should go through governance/audit paths, not UI/UX execution.

cases.userPrompt: Use riss-governance. Evaluate external skill source safety before extraction.
cases.expectedReason: External source safety audits should remain in governance/safety workflows without direct UI/UX execution.
